lessons.title: Multipart Upload Error — Wrong MIME Type or File Path
lessons.context: Multipart file uploads require the correct MIME type to match the actual file format. Using a generic type like 'application/octet-stream' for Excel files causes the server to reject the upload. File paths must also be absolute — relative paths fail depending on where the test runner is invoked.

problem.cause: Incorrect MIME type for the file format, or relative file path that resolves incorrectly

antipattern.description: Using generic MIME type or relative path
antipattern.why_bad: Server validates MIME type against file content — mismatch causes rejection. Relative paths break when test runner CWD differs.
code: // BAD — wrong MIME type + relative path

solution.description: Use the correct MIME type for the file format and always use absolute paths via path.resolve().
code: // GOOD — correct MIME type + absolute path

lessons.ai_instruction: When generating file upload tests, always use path.resolve(__dirname, ...) for file paths and look up the correct MIME type. Common types: .pdf → application/pdf, .xlsx → application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet, .csv → text/csv.
